content:
```json
{
  "provider": "openai",
  "recommendation": "CRUD with audit log",
  "approaches": [
    {
      "name": "CRUD with audit log",
      "pros": [
        "Familiar to the team — Django ORM is purpose-built for CRUD",
        "Simple to implement and maintain",
        "PostgreSQL triggers or Django signals can capture changes for audit",
        "Low learning curve, fast time to market"
      ],
      "cons": [
        "Audit log is a bolt-on, not native to the data model",
        "Harder to replay history or reconstruct past states",
        "May need refactoring if event-driven features grow significantly"
      ],
      "risk_level": "low",
      "fit_rating": "excellent"
    },
    {
      "name": "Event sourcing",
      "pros": [
        "Complete audit trail is inherent — every state change is an event",
        "Natural fit for the future recommendation engine (event stream as input)",
        "Enables temporal queries and state reconstruction"
      ],
      "cons": [
        "Steep learning curve for a team with no ES experience",
        "Requires new infrastructure (event store, projections, possibly CQRS)",
        "Django ORM is not designed for event sourcing — fighting the framework",
        "Significantly more complex for 1000 orders/day which doesn't demand it"
      ],
      "risk_level": "high",
      "fit_rating": "poor"
    }
  ],
  "summary": "For a team of 3 with Django expertise and no event sourcing experience, CRUD with a dedicated audit log table is the pragmatic choice. The order volume (1000/day) doesn't justify the complexity of event sourcing. If the recommendation engine later needs an event stream, you can publish domain events from the CRUD layer without fully adopting event sourcing."
}
```

```json
{
  "provider": "anthropic",
  "recommendation": "CRUD with domain events",
  "approaches": [
    {
      "name": "CRUD with domain events",
      "pros": [
        "Keeps the familiar Django CRUD model the team knows",
        "Domain events (published via Celery) provide an event stream for the recommendation engine",
        "Audit trail via a changes table with before/after snapshots",
        "Incremental path — can evolve toward event sourcing later if needed"
      ],
      "cons": [
        "Dual-write concern: must ensure both the CRUD update and event publish succeed",
        "Audit trail is still a secondary concern, not the primary data model",
        "Domain events need schema versioning as the system evolves"
      ],
      "risk_level": "low",
      "fit_rating": "good"
    },
    {
      "name": "Event sourcing",
      "pros": [
        "Audit trail and event stream are first-class citizens",
        "Natural fit for temporal queries and state reconstruction",
        "Recommendation engine gets a native event stream"
      ],
      "cons": [
        "Team has no ES experience — high ramp-up cost",
        "Django and PostgreSQL are not natural ES tools",
        "CQRS adds operational complexity (projections, eventual consistency)",
        "Over-engineered for current scale"
      ],
      "risk_level": "high",
      "fit_rating": "poor"
    },
    {
      "name": "Plain CRUD",
      "pros": [
        "Simplest possible approach",
        "Team can deliver quickly"
      ],
      "cons": [
        "No audit trail without additional work",
        "No event stream for future recommendation engine",
        "May require significant rework later"
      ],
      "risk_level": "medium",
      "fit_rating": "fair"
    }
  ],
  "summary": "CRUD with domain events strikes the best balance: it leverages the team's Django expertise, provides an audit trail, and creates the event stream the recommendation engine will need — all without the complexity of full event sourcing. Use Django signals to publish order events to a Celery queue, and store audit snapshots in a dedicated table. This positions you to evolve toward event sourcing incrementally if scale demands it."
}
```
